title: Staff Nurse (Central / Private Clinic / Office hours)
description:
Company Profile

We are one of the most trusted names in breast care, diagnostics, and advanced breast surgery. This premier specialist centre is committed to delivering compassionate, comprehensive, and cutting-edge care for every patient who walks through our doors.

Our multidisciplinary team includes renowned breast surgeons, radiologists, breast care nurses, and allied health professionals, all working together in a modern, purpose-built facility designed to provide an exceptional patient experience.

Be part of a respected, forward-thinking institution making a real impact in women’s health. If you're seeking meaningful work, a nurturing team, and an environment where quality truly matters, this opportunity is for you.

Work location: Central

Working hours: 5.5 days work week - Monday to Friday and half Sat, office hours

Key Responsibilities:

Patient Care & Clinical Support

  • Schedule patient appointments and make reminder calls. Manage patient enquiries via phone, email, and messaging platforms.
  • Assist the doctor during consultations and clinic procedures.
  • Scrub in with the doctor to Operating Theatre when required.
  • Arrange radiology exams, laboratory tests, and other investigations; track reports for follow-up visits.
  • Provide clear instructions to patients regarding ordered tests and procedures.
  • Coordinate surgical bookings, including operation theatre, ward admission, anaesthetists, frozen section services, and other required arrangements.
  • Conduct financial counselling for patients undergoing procedures and surgeries by giving estimated fee breakdowns.
  • Liaise with insurance companies to raise pre-authorisations and Letters of Guarantee.
  • Maintain consistent communication with patients undergoing treatment, offering guidance and support.

Administrative & Operational Management

  • Register and bill patient visits accurately.
  • Submit outpatient claims through TPA portals.
  • Manage clinic inventory, supplies, and equipment.
  • Coordinate with external vendors for purchasing, maintenance, and service support.

Key Requirements:

  • Min. Degree or Diploma in Nursing
  • Min. 2-3 years’ experience as a Staff Nurse in clinical settings
  • Must be a Registered Nurse with Singapore Nursing Board (SNB)
  • BCLS & AED Certified
  • Good interpersonal and communication skills
  • Caring and compassionate nature

📩 Your recruiter: WhatsApp your CV/resume to Celine at 8977 7051 for a quicker response.

You may also drop your resume via email to celinechan@envirodynamics.com.sg – only shortlisted candidates will be notified.


Celine Chan I Reg. No: R21103433

EnviroDynamics Solutions Pte Ltd I EA License No.: 12C6285

EnviroDynamics Solutions Pte Ltd was establised in 2008 primarily as engineering consultancy provider in which we have a pool of qualified engineers/consultants offering engineering solutions to our cients.

In addition, we also provide a range of recruitment and human capital solutions as employment agency to our clients in engineering and technology industries.
